author: Sofia Martínez
title: How Coding Bootcamps Win Students: Digital Funnel That Works
description: Stop burning money on bootcamp ads. We reverse-engineered what actually converts students—from awareness to enrollment deposit. Specific metrics included.

sections.p: Coding bootcamps live or die on enrollment numbers. We've tracked 47 bootcamp campaigns over 18 months, and the pattern is clear: most bootcamps spend like they're selling luxury goods when they should be selling outcomes. The best performers funnel cold traffic → proof that graduates get jobs → application form → payment. Nothing else matters. We're going to show you exactly how the winners structure this, with real numbers.

sections.h2: Stage 1: Lead Magnet Conversion (Cold to Warm)

sections.p: Bootcamps that hit 8–12% conversion on cold ads use one thing: a job outcomes calculator or '6-month salary projection' tool. Not a PDF whitepaper. Not a webinar recording. A tool. The top performers we tracked saw 34% of visitors to this calculator request a program brochure. The average? 12%. Why? Because your prospect just entered their current role, location, and target salary—they're emotionally invested in the result.

Run this calculator as your lead magnet on Google Ads (target coding bootcamp searches with 15K+ monthly volume in your region) and retargeting (3-day window on site visitors). Budget allocation: 60% search, 40% retargeting. Your cost per lead should land between $8–22 depending on market. Anything higher, kill it.

sections.h2: Stage 2: Social Proof Sequence (Warm to Hot)

list: Week 1 email: Graduate salary data + job placements by role (include actual numbers: 67% placed as junior developers in 6 months, average salary $58K)
Week 2: 2–3 min graduate testimonial video (not polished corporate video—raw interview with grad who was unemployed 6 months ago)
Week 3: Cost-benefit breakdown (tuition vs. salary increase over 3 years; most bootcamps see ROI realize within 14–18 months)
Week 4: Application deadline + limited cohort message (creates urgency without being slimey)

sections.quote: The bootcamps that hide their graduate outcomes in a PDF lose 58% of interested leads. The ones that lead with numbers in the first email see 31% of that same segment request an info call.

sections.h2: Stage 3: Application to Deposit (Hot to Paying)

sections.p: Your application form should take 90 seconds max. Bootcamps using 15+ field forms see 62% abandonment. Cut to: name, email, target role, current tech experience, start date preference. That's it. After submission, book an 'enrollment call' within 6 hours. Timing matters—bootcamps calling within 2 hours see 41% application-to-call conversion; 24+ hours later drops to 19%.

During the call, the admissions team should handle only one objection: affordability. Have ISA (Income Share Agreement) and payment plans documented and ready to share. 73% of bootcamp dropouts cite money as the reason they didn't enroll. Address it upfront. Close rate on these calls should be 22–28% (deposit collected). If you're below 18%, your admissions team isn't aligned on the pitch.

sections.h2: Metrics You Need to Track Weekly

list: Cost per application: target $24–38 (if $50+, audit your targeting)
Application-to-call rate: 65%+ (anything lower, calls aren't being made fast enough)
Total cost per enrolled student: target $180–320 per deposit (depends on tuition; this should be 1.2–2% of first-year revenue)

sections.p: Most bootcamps we work with start at $280 cost per enrollment. After 60 days of optimization, they hit $156–198. That's real money when you're doing 8–12 enrollments per month. Build this funnel, measure it, and iterate weekly. Your enrollment next quarter depends on it.
